assertions.expectation: Addresses Istio control plane version compatibility with the target Kubernetes version
assertions.reason: Response explicitly addresses version compatibility with section 'Version Compatibility Check' and states 'Istio typically supports Kubernetes N through N-2. If you're on Istio 1.19+, you should have K8s 1.32 support, but verify this first.'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the Istio-Kubernetes version compatibility matrix before upgrading GKE
assertions.reason: Response includes specific command and link: 'Check Istio supported platform matrix: https://istio.io/latest/docs/releases/supported-releases/' in the Version Compatibility Check section

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading Istio first if the current Istio version doesn't support the target Kubernetes version
assertions.reason: Response clearly states the golden rule: 'Control plane first, then data plane, then GKE nodes' and emphasizes 'Never upgrade GKE and Istio simultaneously — always upgrade Istio first'

assertions.expectation: Explains that sidecar injection happens via mutating webhook and may cause issues during node drain/recreation
assertions.reason: Response addresses webhook failures as a common pitfall with specific section 'Admission Webhook Failures' including commands to check and fix mutating webhook configurations

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new pods on upgraded nodes will get sidecar injected — verify the sidecar version matches
assertions.reason:
Response includes specific commands to verify sidecar versions: 'kubectl get pods -n STAGING_NAMESPACE -o jsonpath='{range .items[*]}{.metadata.name}{"	"}{.metadata.annotations.sidecar\.istio\.io/proxyImage}{"
"}{end}'' and discusses sidecar migration process

assertions.expectation: Recommends monitoring Istio proxy version consistency across the mesh after upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes mesh-specific monitoring section with commands like 'istioctl proxy-status' and emphasizes monitoring 'Istio proxy version consistency across the mesh' in the key takeaways

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check Istio version and proxy sync status (istioctl or kubectl)
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple specific commands including 'kubectl get pods -n istio-system -l app=istiod -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.labels.istio\.io/rev}'' and 'istioctl proxy-status' throughout the document

assertions.expectation: Warns about potential traffic disruption if Istio control plane and sidecar versions are mismatched
assertions.reason: Response warns about mesh connectivity issues and includes monitoring for 'mTLS connections failing' and 'Split-Brain During Node Upgrades' as specific pitfalls, emphasizing the importance of version consistency
